results.evidence: adversarial_common/gates.py (sibling skill at /home/chpo/.hermes/skills/adversarial-common, imported via sys.path.insert in scripts/adversarial_plan.py:35) has an uncommitted working-tree change to _DEFAULT_THRESHOLDS: the 'spec' profile's required_sections changed from ["Requirements"] to [] (gates.py:20). Since check_context's required-section loop (gates.py:59-63) iterates effective['required_sections'], and that list is now empty for kind='spec', the loop never executes and no heading is required at all — the exact-heading gate described in the finding no longer exists. The file's mtime (2026-07-15 19:22) is after the last commit touching scripts/adversarial_plan.py (19:11), consistent with this being the fix applied for this finding. The pre-flight call site itself, ctx = gates.check_context("spec", spec_text) in scripts/adversarial_plan.py (line shifted from the reported 234 due to earlier diff insertions), is unchanged and still runs, but it now imposes no section-heading requirement, so arbitrary spec.md files (with 'Functional Requirements', 'Goals', or no such heading at all) will pass as long as they meet the length/token minimums.
